summary: Crisp soundboard capture of an energetic April ’83 show highlighted by a monster Scarlet>Fire and stellar Brent keyboard jams.
review: The recording is a solid sound‑board source that sounds balanced and clear, though listeners may need to turn the volume up and note a slight echo on some sections. Musically the concert shines – Jerry’s inventive lead work and Phil’s blazing bass drive a half‑hour of Scarlet>Fire, while Brent’s analog keyboards add a laid‑back groove to new songs like West LA, Esau and Maybe You Know. The intimate college gym setting fuels an energetic second set that many fans recall as a classic early‑80s experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars and praise both the performance (standout jams, strong playing) and the recording (crisp, balanced sound), outweighing minor volume/echo issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and balanced overall; some reviewers note you have to turn the volume up, a slight echo in parts, and a brief audience splice (3:44‑9:42) but no major dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic, classic early‑80s set with a memorable second set; new material is played confidently and the intimate gym atmosphere adds to the show’s momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for blazing, inventive guitar work and strong lead playing throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as ‘blazing’ alongside Jerry, indicating a solid bass foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Fans remark on his quirky spoken moments at the end of the first set (hillbilly accent, funny banter).
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s analog keyboard jams are highlighted as some of his best for this period, adding groovy texture.
